title: PV Sindhu's Early Steps to Stardom in Macau
indian_angle: This story focuses on the formative years of India's premier female shuttler, PV Sindhu, and her journey to becoming a global star.
summary: A look back at PV Sindhu's initial journey in badminton, tracing her first steps towards stardom in Macau, a significant location in her early career.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reunites for Asian Games; Treesa-Gayatri Back
indian_angle: The article details the reassembly of India's successful Thomas Cup squad for the upcoming Asian Games, highlighting the return of key players Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: India's badminton squad, including members of the victorious Thomas Cup team, is reassembling for the Asian Games, with the notable return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: More Like Saina Nehwal or PV Sindhu?
indian_angle: This piece analyzes the playing style of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma, drawing comparisons to legendary Indian shuttlers Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: An analytical look into the development of rising Indian badminton player Tanvi Sharma's game, comparing her style and potential trajectory to that of Indian legends Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.

title: Indian Shuttlers Reveal FIFA World Cup Team Preferences
indian_angle: This fun article reveals which national football teams top Indian badminton players like Chirag Shetty, HS Prannoy, Ayush Shetty, and PV Sindhu are supporting in the FIFA World Cup.
summary: Top Indian badminton players, including Chirag Shetty, HS Prannoy, Ayush Shetty, and PV Sindhu, share their favourite teams for the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026.

title: Tan Kim Her's Strategy: Pairing Hari-Arjun After Satwik-Chirag
indian_angle: The article delves into the coaching philosophy of Tan Kim Her, specifically how he fostered the new Indian doubles pair of Hariharan and Arjun, following the success of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.
summary: Doubles coach Tan Kim Her's strategic approach to building Indian doubles pairs is explored, focusing on his role in creating the Hariharan-Arjun partnership after the successful formation of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.

title: PV Sindhu Needs More Self-Belief to Overcome An Se-young
indian_angle: This opinion piece critically examines what PV Sindhu needs to improve mentally to consistently challenge and defeat her formidable opponent, An Se-young.
summary: An analysis suggesting that PV Sindhu's path to defeating the dominant An Se-young lies in cultivating greater self-belief and mental fortitude on court.
